title: [lora_l2.c:184] rx_thread does not check aborted state in loop
description: Function: rx_thread (static). Thread safety: The RX thread loop condition (line 218) only checks 'atomic_get(&lora_state.running)'. If the module enters aborted state (lora_state.aborted = 1) but running is not cleared, the thread continues. In practice, stop() sets running=0 before or after setting aborted=1, so this is a non-issue. But checking aborted in the loop condition would make the thread exit faster when abort is signaled. Priority 3 - theoretical race, not a practical bug.
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation comment explaining that checking LORA_RUNNING is sufficient because LORA_ABORTED can only be set after the RX thread is already terminated. The shutdown sequence in stop() is: (1) transition to STOPPED, (2) join/abort thread, (3) only then transition to ABORTED. So the thread exits gracefully on STOPPED or is forcibly terminated before ABORTED is ever set.
